content.problemStatement:
Max is an avid sauna visitor. Today he's chilling in the sauna with nnn friends. Max knows that each friend is going to visit the steam room exactly once, and the iii-th friend is going to enter the steam room at time lil_ili​, and leave at time rir_iri​ (time is measured in seconds since the start of the visit). Max also wants to make a single visit to the steam room, but he can still choose his entering and leaving time.

Max has his heat endurance reputation to maintain. In people's minds, a person AAA is cooler than another person BBB if AAA enters the steam room strictly earlier than BBB, but leaves strictly later than BBB (thus proving that he's able to withstand heat longer). Depending on when Max enters and leaves the steam room, his cred will be equal to the number of people that are less cool than Max, minus the number of people that are cooler than Max.

Max has his limits, and so he cannot spend more than ttt seconds in the steam room. Help Max determine the maximum cred he can get if he chooses entering and leaving times optimally. Note that Max's entering and leaving times do not have to be integers, and are even allowed to be negative.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. TTT descriptions of test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two integers nnn and ttt, denoting the number of Max's friends, and the maximum time Max can spend in the steam room, respectively.
The following nnn lines describe entering and leaving time of Max's friends. The iii-th of these lines contains two integers lil_ili​ and rir_iri​ - the entering and leaving time of the iii-th friend.


Output Format
For each test case, on a separate line print a single integer, the maximum cred Max can get while spending at most ttt seconds in the steam room.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers n and t, where n is the number of friends and t is the maximum time Max can spend in the steam room. The following n lines describe the entering and leaving times of Max's friends, with each line containing two integers li and ri.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the maximum cred Max can get while spending at most t seconds in the steam room.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first sample case Max may enter at time 0.5 and leave at time 9.5, thus becoming cooler than all of his friends.
In the second sample Max cannot simultaneously be cooler than everyone, but, for example, if he enters at time 0.3 and leaves at time 7.1, he will be cooler than friends 1 and 2, and incomparable with friend 3.
In the third sample Max can only hope to be cooler than friend 2, but in all such cases he will be out-cooled by friend 1, thus he cannot gain positive cred.
In the fourth sample, Max can get cred 0 by entering and leaving before or after all of his friends.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to find the optimal entering and leaving times for Max to maximize his cred. We can iterate over all possible entering times and calculate the corresponding leaving time based on the maximum time Max can spend in the steam room. For each entering time, we count the number of friends that are less cool than Max and the number of friends that are cooler than Max. We update the maximum cred if the current cred is higher.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n * t) — the time complexity is dominated by the iteration over all possible entering times and the counting of friends for each entering time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) — the space complexity is dominated by the storage of the entering and leaving times of Max's friends.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    n, t = map(int, input().split())
    li = [0] * n
    ri = [0] * n
    for i in range(n):
        li[i], ri[i] = map(int, input().split())
    maxCred = 0
    for enter in [i * 0.1 for i in range(int(1e9 * 10) + 1)]:
        leave = enter + t
        lessCool = sum(1 for i in range(n) if enter < li[i] and leave > ri[i])
        cooler = sum(1 for i in range(n) if enter > li[i] and leave < ri[i])
        maxCred = max(maxCred, lessCool - cooler)
    print(maxCred)
